raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally involves refining the thesis, gap, and contribution framing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The request entails checking whether the motivation, methodological extension, results, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The draft makes quantitative performance and efficiency claims, so improvement should ensure those claims align with the reported dataset, baseline, and metrics.","R1.5":"A general draft-improvement request includes responsibility for section-level ordering and narrative arc, even though the artifact is short.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because 'Improve this draft' most directly asks for clearer, smoother, more professional prose.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about facts that cannot be verified from the artifact alone.","RX.2":"The projection must include avoiding stronger claims than the artifact’s evidence can support.","RX.3":"The projection must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities and not perform unrelated validation or rewriting work.","RX.4":"The projection must account for avoiding misleading scientific or deployment claims that could affect downstream technical decisions.","RX.5":"The projection must preserve traceability to the provided artifact and distinguish artifact-grounded judgments from assumptions."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally involves refining the thesis, gap, and contribution framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The request entails checking whether the motivation, methodological extension, results, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The draft makes quantitative performance and efficiency claims, so improvement should ensure those claims align with the reported dataset, baseline, and metrics.
rationale.R1.5: A general draft-improvement request includes responsibility for section-level ordering and narrative arc, even though the artifact is short.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because 'Improve this draft' most directly asks for clearer, smoother, more professional prose.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about facts that cannot be verified from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must include avoiding stronger claims than the artifact’s evidence can support.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities and not perform unrelated validation or rewriting work.
rationale.RX.4: The projection must account for avoiding misleading scientific or deployment claims that could affect downstream technical decisions.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must preserve traceability to the provided artifact and distinguish artifact-grounded judgments from assumptions.
